preset_description: Dark = substance signal. Spotlights product capabilities and metrics. CTA and pricing are understated — features are the story, not conversion pressure.

brief.audience: AI-first, intent-aware design system for founding teams

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Establishes what MODE is — an intent-aware design system built for founding teams — and immediately signals relevance to the Validator audience who needs to confirm they're in the right place before investing attention.

beats.name: Value
beats.rationale: With context set, articulate the core capabilities and outcome promises (AI-first components, intent-aware patterns, speed-to-ship for founding teams). Validators need to understand what the product actually does before they'll engage with proof, so Value precedes Credibility and Evidence.

beats.name: Credibility
beats.rationale: Validators require trust signals before committing. Social proof — founding team logos, testimonials, notable backers, or ecosystem endorsements — satisfies their need for external validation and reduces perceived risk at the awareness stage.

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: Concrete metrics, case study data, or performance benchmarks give the Validator the quantitative proof they demand. Placing Evidence after Credibility compounds trust: first who uses it, then what measurable results they achieved.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: Awareness-stage soft CTA — e.g., 'Start exploring free' or 'See it in action' — positioned after proof so the Validator feels justified to act. No pricing or hard commitment; just a low-friction path into the product experience.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Final beat catches visitors not ready to convert with secondary paths: documentation links, a newsletter signup, community access, or social channels — giving the Validator alternative ways to continue validating on their own terms.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.name: Hero
sections.rationale: Establishes what MODE is — an intent-aware design system built for founding teams — and immediately signals relevance to the Validator audience who needs to confirm they're in the right place before investing attention.

sections.name: Social proof
sections.rationale: Validators require trust signals before committing. Social proof — founding team logos, testimonials, notable backers, or ecosystem endorsements — satisfies their need for external validation and reduces perceived risk at the awareness stage.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: Awareness stage funnel requires full variant for 'exploration and top-funnel pages' to establish broad context and wayfinding for organic traffic discovering MODE.
Awareness funnel stage prefers 'editorial variant is the high-expression awareness layout' to earn attention and establish intent-aware design system context above the fold.

slots.headline: Your design system knows how to render. MODE teaches it why.
slots.subhead: MODE resolves component behavior, copy register, and visual emphasis to the right experience for every archetype and funnel stage — without manual variants or QA cycles.
slots.trust_signal: One-time purchase. Lifetime access. Lives in your codebase.

page.reasoning: Validator archetype prefers variants containing 'social-proof'; 'Validator variant leads with quote, logos below' to establish credibility before conversion consideration.

slots.headline: Built for teams who ship in code, not Figma handoffs.
slots.quote: We had a working intelligence layer before our first standup. MODE resolved what would have taken us weeks of variant QA into a single configured preset.
slots.attribution: Founding engineer, early-access cohort — internal feedback, 2024

page.reasoning: Standard variant is appropriate for awareness stage awareness pages; 'standard is the default' approach without premature conversion-stage CTA recovery tactics.
